frame_analyses.description: Title screen showing 'Intracorporeal Suture' - task has not begun
View of laparoscopic trainer setup from external angle
Laparoscopic view showing two needle drivers positioned over Penrose drain with suture markings
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both instruments in view, needle not yet loaded

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle at appropriate position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp at 2/3 point from tip, proper orientation

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned to pierce first suture mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle approaching mark 1, good angle of entry

frame_analyses.description: Needle being passed through second mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears accurate through both marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning intracorporeal knot tying - first throw formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right instrument leading, proper surgeon's knot technique initiated

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed with left instrument leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand switching technique, maintaining tension

frame_analyses.description: Final throw with right instrument leading again
Task completed - instruments withdrawn, suture in place

penalties.description: Unable to precisely measure suture deviation from marks due to camera angle and lighting

knot_assessments.notes: Final throw completed with appropriate tension

confidence_rationale: Video shows clear task execution with good technique demonstration. However, precise penalty assessment is limited by camera angles and lighting conditions, particularly for measuring exact suture deviation distances.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation due to oblique camera angles
Precise timing due to video editing and cuts between phases
Final suture tail cutting as video ends before this phase
technique_summary: Expert-level performance with excellent timing (120s vs 80-120s expert benchmark). Clean knot sequence with proper hand switching and good instrument technique throughout.
strengths: Consistent hand switching between all throws
improvement_suggestions: Video quality limits detailed assessment - higher resolution would allow more precise evaluation
